test.name: Negative result should be logged with enough detail to support exhaustiveness
test.description: Search full-text for a Flynn family member in a context where they're not expected to appear (e.g., a probate record). The fixture returns no matches. Tests negative-result handling: 'no results' on its own is not useful for the GPS exhaustive-search claim. The log must record what was searched, in which collections, with which queries — so a future exhaustive-search declaration can cite this search as evidence of due diligence.

input.user_message: Search for Patrick Flynn as a beneficiary or executor in Schuylkill County probate records — full-text search.

judge_context: Notes on the log entry should describe what collections and date-ranges were covered, not just record that the search ran — the GPS audit trail needs to know WHAT was searched, not just that nothing came up
Should not fabricate results to fill in a perceived gap — negative is a legitimate outcome and must be honestly recorded
Should suggest at least one variant query the user could try if they wanted to widen the search (alternative spelling, broader date range, different collection)
